summary: Great‑sounding audience tape captures a fiery fall ’72 set with extended jams on Playin’, Truckin’ and The Other One plus an impassioned Stella Blue and a plucky Stars & Stripes encore.
review: The three reviewers all praise the show’s hot, long jams – especially the stretched‑out Playin’, Truckin’ and The Other One – and note a particularly heartfelt rendition of Stella Blue and a brief Stars & Stripes after the encore.  The audience recording is generally clear and lively, though quiet passages mute the keyboards slightly.  Overall the performance is rated as an excellent example of a fall‑1972 Dead show.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (extended jams, strong vocal performances) and recording is described as great overall with only minor muffling of keyboards, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great overall clarity and lively ambience; louder sections come through very well.  Quiet passages – especially the keyboards during Playin' – are a bit mushy, causing some loss of detail.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert flows with long, hot jams that build momentum through the first set and carry into an expansive second set; extended improvisations on Playin', Truckin' and The Other One keep energy high, while the brief encore and post‑encore Stars & Stripes provide a satisfying close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry’s guitar is prominent throughout, contributing to the hot jams and the plucky Stars & Stripes finale.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil is absent for most of the show, with reviewers noting his disappearance after the encore.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (Bobby) is mentioned as part of the post‑encore jam but receives no specific critique.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards are audible in louder sections but get lost in the mush during quiet moments.
